result.summary: oracle.com exposes Oracle Cloud Infrastructure REST APIs, the OCI CLI, and Oracle database-oriented MCP/GraphQL surfaces including Autonomous AI Database MCP, SQLcl MCP, and ORDS GraphQL.
result.description: Oracle provides cloud infrastructure, database, and enterprise software platforms. Its public documentation covers OCI service APIs and developer tooling, plus database access surfaces such as Oracle REST Data Services GraphQL and MCP servers for Oracle databases.

oci_api_signing_key.label: OCI API signing key + OCIDs
oci_api_signing_key.setup: Create an OCI API signing key for your user and gather the required tenancy and user OCIDs as described in [Request Signatures](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/signingrequests.htm). Use the linked key/OCID setup referenced there, then configure your client or CLI profile with the key fingerprint, private key, tenancy OCID, user OCID, and region.

oci_cli_session.label: OCI CLI session token
oci_cli_session.setup: Install the OCI CLI, then run `oci session authenticate` as described in [Token-based Authentication for the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/SDKDocs/clitoken.htm). Complete the browser sign-in flow and the CLI writes a configuration file with a temporary session token for subsequent `oci` commands.

variables.name: region
variables.description: Regional OCI API hostname segment, for example `us-ashburn-1`.

variables.name: apiVersion
variables.description: Service API version in the request path, for example `20160918`.

variables.name: region-identifier
variables.description: Region identifier for the Autonomous AI Database.

variables.name: database-ocid
variables.description: OCID of the Autonomous AI Database with MCP enabled.

variables.name: HOST
variables.description: Host serving Oracle REST Data Services.

variables.name: PORT
variables.description: Port serving Oracle REST Data Services.

variables.name: Schema
variables.description: REST-enabled Oracle schema exposed through ORDS.
